availability.summary: Live probes on 2026-05-10 returned 200 responses for state-level obligation and outlay geography aggregations and returned documented validation errors for bad def_codes requests.

sources.title: Current raw MCP profile for disaster spending_by_geography
Derived MCP coverage check for disaster spending_by_geography
Live USAspending probes for disaster spending_by_geography on 2026-05-10

parameters.description: Primary disaster filter object used before the geography aggregation is computed.

documented.notes: The staged request schema block says string, but the attribute table and examples describe an object payload.

constraints: Additional shared search-filter keys may also be accepted by this endpoint.

observed.notes: Live probes confirmed the endpoint expects a JSON object body with nested filter content, not a raw string payload.

parameters.description: One or more Disaster Emergency Fund Codes (DEFC) whose matching disaster spending should be aggregated.

documented.notes: If the DEFC values are in the COVID-19 group, the docs say transactions are limited to action_date on or after 2020-04-01.

constraints: At least one DEFC is required.
Codes are case-sensitive uppercase values from the published DEFC set.

observed.notes: Empty arrays returned HTTP 422 and lowercase l returned HTTP 400 with the valid uppercase DEFC enum list.

parameters.description: Optional award-type filter applied before geography aggregation.
constraints: Use USAspending award type codes, not plain-language categories.

parameters.description: Optional shared search-filter date ranges passed through the disaster filter object.

documented.notes: This shape comes from the current raw profile's shared Search Filters handling rather than the staged endpoint markdown.

constraints: Each item should include start_date and end_date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
date_type may further specify which date dimension to filter.

parameters.description: Optional shared search-filter toggle used to constrain recipient geography scope before aggregation.

documented.notes: The current raw profile lists observed values domestic and foreign.

constraints: Use only recognized recipient scope tokens such as domestic or foreign.

parameters.description: Optional list of recipient location objects used before the geography rollup is calculated.

documented.notes: The current raw profile gives examples like {"country":"USA","state":"CA"}.

constraints: Location-object structure follows the shared USAspending search filters pattern.

parameters.description: Requested geographic aggregation level for the response rows.
constraints: The selected layer determines the meaning of shape_code and geo_layer_filters values.

observed.notes: Only the state layer was live-probed in this run; county and district remain documented but unverified here.

parameters.description: Optional allowlist of shape codes currently in view or otherwise selected for the chosen geography layer.
constraints: Use this to limit results to specific visible states, counties, or districts.

observed.notes: Filtering to CA and TX returned exactly those two state rows in the live outlay probe.

parameters.description: Chooses whether spending is grouped by recipient location or place of performance geography.
constraints: Omitting scope is documented to default to recipient_location.

parameters.description: Selects which financial measure is summed for each geography row.
constraints: The chosen measure changes the meaning of the amount field in the results.

observed.notes: face_value_of_loan remains documented but was not re-probed in this run.

templates.name: state_obligation_default_scope
templates.description: Minimal live-validated state-level obligation request. Omitting scope defaults to recipient_location.

templates.name: filtered_state_outlay_place_of_performance
templates.description: Live-validated outlay request limited to selected states and grouped by place of performance.

response.shapeSummary: Returns a single JSON object with echoed geo_layer, scope, and spending_type plus a results array. Each row is one geography aggregate for the selected layer; when geo_layer_filters are omitted, live probes showed an additional uncoded bucket with null shape_code and display_name.

fields.description: Echo of the requested aggregation layer.
Echo of the geographic scope used for aggregation.
Echo of the summed spending measure.
Array of aggregated geography rows for the chosen layer.
constraints: No page_metadata or page controls are documented for this response.

observed.notes: The unfiltered state obligation probe returned a full nationwide results array including territories and an uncoded bucket; the filtered outlay probe returned exactly two rows for CA and TX.

fields.description: Geographic identifier for the row; current profile evidence suggests null can appear for an uncoded aggregate bucket when geography assignment is missing.
constraints: Interpret the code using the selected geo_layer: state postal code, county FIPS-like code, or district code.

observed.notes: The live baseline response included a null shape_code row for an uncoded aggregate bucket.

fields.description: Summed dollar amount for the requested spending_type.

observed.notes: Negative amounts are possible; the live baseline included a negative obligation total for New Jersey.

fields.description: Human-readable geography label; current profile evidence suggests it can be null for an uncoded aggregate bucket.

observed.notes: The live baseline response included a null display_name row for the uncoded aggregate bucket.

fields.description: Population associated with the returned geography, if available.
Per-capita spending amount derived from amount and population when population is available.
Count of awards contributing to the aggregated amount for the geography row.

pagination.notes: The endpoint returns one full results array with no documented paging controls or page metadata.

notes: Nested disaster filter fields are necessary for a coding agent to construct valid requests, but a top-level-only tool surface obscures them.
The endpoint has no pagination, so request-construction value comes mostly from nested filter guidance and enum semantics.

contradictions.statement: The staged markdown's schema block says the POST body is a string, but live requests and the request example require a JSON object payload.
contradictions.impact: Agents should send an object body with filter, geo_layer, and spending_type rather than a raw string.

contradictions.statement: The documentation marks results.shape_code and results.display_name as required strings, but the live baseline response included null values for an uncoded aggregate bucket.
contradictions.impact: Callers must handle null geography identifiers and labels in addition to named geography rows.

quirks.statement: Omitting scope returned recipient_location in the live baseline response.
quirks.impact: Set scope explicitly when you need place_of_performance geography.

quirks.statement: Filtering to specific state shape codes returned only those rows and removed the uncoded null bucket in the sampled filtered request.
quirks.impact: geo_layer_filters can be used to keep map-driven requests small and focused.

quirks.statement: Negative geography totals are possible; the live baseline included a negative obligation amount for New Jersey.
quirks.impact: Do not assume amount is non-negative when ranking or formatting results.

gaps.statement: This run reverified only the state layer; county and district behavior remain documented but not re-probed here.
The live pass did not re-test face_value_of_loan or the shared filter passthrough fields carried in the current raw profile.

risks.statement: Because geography rows are aggregates with no award identifiers, analysts can misread the endpoint as award-level data unless they explicitly plan a drill-down workflow.
risks.impact: Use a second endpoint for award-level detail after identifying relevant geographies here.

risks.statement: Bad def_codes requests did not fail uniformly: an empty array returned 422 while lowercase input returned 400.
risks.impact: Clients should handle more than one validation status family.
